description: Memory management kernel functions for resource observability

answers: why is my container being killed

fields.name: total_vm
fields.meaning: total virtual memory of the victim

probes.use_when: containers are being killed unexpectedly. OOM kills are the kernel's last resort when memory is exhausted — the cgroup limit was hit or the node ran out of memory.
probes.not_when: you're looking for gradual memory growth — this only fires when the kernel kills a process. Use memory metrics for trending.

interpretations.pattern: oom_kill fires for a specific container repeatedly
interpretations.conclusion: container memory limit is too low, or the application has a memory leak
interpretations.action: check container memory limits. if the limit is reasonable, investigate the application for memory leaks.

interpretations.pattern: oom_kill fires for multiple different processes
interpretations.conclusion: node-level memory exhaustion — not a single container's fault
interpretations.action: check node memory pressure. too many pods scheduled on this node.

answers: which process is allocating the most memory
probes.use_when: you suspect memory allocation failures. very high frequency — always use sampling (sample_rate: 0.01 or lower). ret == 0 means allocation failed.
probes.not_when: you need cgroup memory usage — use cgroup metrics. this fires on every page allocation, which is millions per second.

interpretations.conclusion: page allocation failing — system is under severe memory pressure
interpretations.action: the kernel cannot allocate memory. this precedes OOM kills. check node memory immediately.
no action. use for baseline allocation rate per process.

answers: is a cgroup hitting its memory limit
probes.use_when: you need to know when a container's memory limit is being enforced by the kernel. ret != 0 means the cgroup memory charge was rejected.
probes.not_when: you need aggregate cgroup memory usage — use cgroup metrics.

interpretations.conclusion: cgroup memory limit is being hit repeatedly — container is at its memory ceiling
interpretations.action: increase container memory limit or investigate application memory usage.

interpretations.conclusion: memory charge rejection leading to OOM kill — the cgroup has no reclaim headroom
interpretations.action: container memory limit is too low. increase limit or reduce application memory footprint.
